stem:
Refer to the following letter and symbol series and answer the question.
(Left) E ≠ R & H & @ J & M N ÷ B & V && D ≠ P @ W % & Q & A % & ≠ E & (Right)
If all the symbols are dropped from the series, which of the following will be third from the left?
Let 0° < θ < 90°, √((sec²θ + cosec²θ)/(tan²θ − sin²θ)) × ((1 + tan²θ) sin²θ)/((cosecθ − cotθ)(1 + cosθ)) is equal to:
A solid metallic cuboid of sides 4 cm × 16 cm × 17 cm is melted and made into small cubes of sides 2 cm × 2 cm × 2 cm. How many such cubes can be made?

Sharad walks 4 km towards the west and then turns right and walks 5 km. Again, he turns left and walks 7 km. Finally, he turns to his left and walks 9 km. How far is he from his starting point?
(All turns are 90-degree turns only.)

A certain number of people are sitting in a row, facing north. B sits fourth to the right of E. C sits third to the right of D. A sits sixth to the left of E. There are only two persons between C and D. If no other person is sitting in the row, what is the total number of persons present?
How many prime numbers are there between 20 and 80?
Which of the following wastes CANNOT be degraded by bacteria or other saprophytes?
In a quadrilateral ABCD, ∠A = 86° and ∠B = 72°. The bisectors of ∠C and ∠D meet at O. What is the measure of ∠DOC?

Six friends A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting in a circle facing the centre of the circle. A is 2nd to the right of B. Only one person sits between F and D. B and D are immediate neighbours. F is 2nd to the right of D. Who sits between D and F?
What is the main role of the Reserve Bank of India?
options: It conducts consolidated supervision of the financial sector in India.
It provides fund to the private sector industries.
It maintains the profit generated by the banking sector.
It lends money to all people.

stem: Which of the following is an indigenously developed automatic train protection system tested in India in March 2022?

Match the following:
Compound — Name
i. C2H6 — a. Ethane
ii. H2C=CH2 — b. Benzene
iii. CH3CHO — c. Acetaldehyde
iv. CH3COCH3 — d. Ethylene
v. CH3CH3 — e. Acetone

Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.
158, 157, 153, 174, 158, ?
Which of the following statements about the Rajya Sabha is correct?
options: Half of the members retire every year.
One-third of the members retire every second year.
One-fifth of the members retire every second year.
One-third of the members retire every year.

stem: In March 2022, in which state did the World Health Organization sign an agreement with India to set up a Global Centre for Traditional Medicine?
Who gave the laws of inheritance?
The volume of a cube whose side is 12 cm is:
Which of the following features of the Constitution of India is borrowed from the Constitution of Ireland?
A 2200 W electric toaster operates at 220 V. The resistance of its coil is:
In the process of storing and transferring ADP into ATP molecules, which of the following minerals is critical?

If 2 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 485136765, what will be the difference between the digits which are second from the left and third from the right?
In a certain code language, CARBON is coded as DCQFIT and ENABLE is coded as FPDFQK, then how is CANDLE coded in that language?
AB is parallel to CD. A transversal PQ intersects AB and CD at E and F, respectively, and ∠PEB = 70°. G is a point between AB and CD such that ∠HEG = 18° and ∠GFE = 50°. What is the measure of ∠EGF?

Given below is a statement followed by two possible conclusions I and II. Read the information carefully and select the correct option.
Statement: Country B has been importing 80% of its crude oil and petroleum from country Y for a few decades now to fulfil their fuel needs. However, owing to the current financial crisis in country Y, it expects the crude oil and petroleum to other countries at 20% higher prices now.
Conclusions:
I. The crude industry in country B will be on verge of a total shut down in the coming months.
II. Country B will instruct fuel use by 50%.
Which of the following can be concluded from the given information?
options: Neither I nor II can be concluded from the given information.
Both I and II can be concluded from the given information.
Only II can be concluded from the given information.
Only I can be concluded from the given information.

stem: In July 2022, the Supreme Court of India gave an order giving more power to the right to be forgotten, which has been acknowledged as a facet of the right to privacy by the top court in its 2017 landmark judgment, the order is related to ______.
On which national festival is the National Flag of India hoisted by the Prime Minister at the Red Fort in old Delhi?

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows.
A F B C D S Y E N T D D C 2 D Q A C V Y U U N G R F S C W B
How many such consonants are there each of which is immediately preceded by a vowel and also immediately followed by a vowel?
If a = 5, then √(4a² − 4a + 1) + 6a is:
